meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Code Like a Pro: Client Workflow for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Master the ultimate client workflow to eliminate debugging roadblocks. Learn intermediate techniques using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your projects.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at red error logs in isolation. By adopting a structured client-side debugging workflow, you transform cryptic stack traces into actionable intelligence. Leverage Antigravity to map dependency logic and Claude Code to perform iterative, autonomous refactoring, effectively turning your debugging sessions into high-velocity learning opportunities for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen that has been blank for three hours, save for a cryptic stack trace that defies logic. The frustration is palpable, and the momentum you built yesterday is evaporating. Every student developer hits this wall—the moment when code stops behaving and starts fighting back.

But here is the secret: professional debugging isn't about intuition; it's about systematic isolation. By shifting to a professional client workflow, you stop guessing and start engineering solutions. We are going to turn your debugging process into a diagnostic engine, leveraging AI to handle the heavy lifting while you master the architecture.

steps.title: State Isolation and Reproduction
steps.description: Before fixing, define the exact state where the error occurs. Use Antigravity to generate a minimal reproduction script that isolates the faulty logic from the rest of your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this component for state inconsistencies. Generate a minimal unit test script that reproduces the current runtime error in isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file ./src/components/buggy_module.ts --task 'Create a test runner to isolate this state error'

steps.title: Automated Dependency Tracing
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map the flow of data through your affected functions. Understanding where the data turns corrupt is 90% of the battle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the data flow for this function. Identify every mutation point and suggest where the null pointer might originate.

steps.title: Iterative Refactoring with Claude Code
steps.description: Once the root cause is identified, use Claude Code to suggest a fix. Always ask for a 'diff' to understand exactly what is changing in your architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the identified bug and draft a refactor that adheres to SOLID principles and prevents this specific regression.

steps.title: Verification and Edge Case Testing
steps.description: Don't just fix the error; ensure the fix doesn't break dependent modules. Use AI to generate edge cases you haven't considered.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate 5 edge-case scenarios for this new function, including boundary values and asynchronous race conditions.

steps.title: Documentation and Knowledge Capture
steps.description: Turn your debugging experience into a documentation asset. This builds your portfolio and makes you faster for the next project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize this debugging session into a technical blog post snippet explaining the problem, the diagnosis, and the architectural fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --doc 'Post-mortem analysis of the recent state bug'

pro_tips: Never accept the first AI suggestion; always ask 'Why is this the optimal approach?' to force deeper technical reasoning.
Use Claude Code to create a 'debug log' file that keeps track of every failed attempt, preventing you from repeating circular logic.
When debugging asynchronous code, ask your tools to specifically check for 'floating promises' that aren't being awaited correctly.
Treat your error logs as data; feed them into Antigravity to identify patterns in your own coding habits over time.
Always commit your 'broken' state to a separate Git branch before starting the AI-assisted fix to maintain a clean rollback point.
resources: MDN Web Docs for Error Handling: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide/Control_flow_and_error_handling
content.next_steps: After mastering this workflow, integrate it into your CI/CD pipeline to automate error detection before your code even hits production.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to dismantle any bug that stands in your way. Stop fearing the error log and start treating it as a roadmap to becoming a senior-level engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
